attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Disorder of eye (sight-threatening red eye — keratitis / acute angle-closure glaucoma / anterior uveitis grouping)

transform_flags: Patient age not stated in the source note; inferred as adult (34) from an independent, working, office-based adult living alone — please confirm on clinician review.
Patient sex not stated in the source note; sex_at_birth inferred as female with no strong source signal — please confirm/correct on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-OPHTH-01-00248) derived from source CIA-048 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-OPHTH-01 bucket at ingest (cases:ingest --reseq).

Specialty assigned OPHTHAL (ophthalmology) per taxonomy; the id carries the 5-char legacy code OPHTH because the id regex allows only [A-Z]{2,6} (the canonical 7-char OPHTHAL cannot fit) — case_metadata.specialty_tags is authoritative, consistent with the existing six SPEC-OPHTH cases.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours consult with slit-lamp-equivalent examination, fluorescein staining, preauricular-node palpation, visual-acuity measurement and clinician-measured vitals. The presentation layer was de-anchored to pre-diagnosis patient wording; clinician-only exam/investigations (preauricular adenopathy, follicular reaction, negative fluorescein, 6/6 acuity, clear corneas/reactive pupils/no ciliary flush) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the source's urgent on-site MBS escalation was reprojected to the clinically-correct low-tier self-care + contagion control + red-flag safety-netting.

Dose recorded for lubricant eye drops (up to 2-hourly as needed) is taken from the source note; the source's 'every 2 hours' lubricant and '4 times daily' cool-compress instructions were preserved. No dose synthesised; no antibiotic/steroid dose recorded as neither is indicated.

SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM candidate codes are best-effort from knowledge and are ALL unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; the sight-threatening-mimic grouping code (301348007) is a placeholder for a composite differential and must be resolved/split by the terminology-verification step. No AMT code asserted for the OTC lubricant (uncertain) — captured as dose only.

Normal clinician-measured vital signs from the source (BP 124/80, HR 76, Temp 37.2, RR 14, SpO2 100%) were not patient-obtained and are not clinically pivotal here; they were not injected into 01 and are treated as sealed/immaterial background.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
